2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Google’s own data shows that as page load time goes from 1 second to 5 seconds, bounce probability jumps by 90%. If your site is slow, Google notices before your visitors even finish waiting.
Most business owners think “performance” means the site looks fine on their own laptop. Google measures it completely differently — load speed, stability, responsiveness, and crawlability, on every page, all the time. Here are the mistakes we see most often, and what actually fixes them.
1. Ignoring Core Web Vitals entirely. Most site owners have never opened PageSpeed Insights. If you don’t know your LCP score, you can’t fix it — and Google penalizes you for it quietly, every day.
2. Bloated, unoptimized images. A single unoptimized hero image can add two to three seconds to mobile load time. In client audits, the Capslock team has found that image compression alone can cut homepage load time by 40–60% without touching the design.
3. Render-blocking JavaScript and CSS. When a browser has to execute a pile of scripts before it can paint the page, visitors stare at a blank screen — and that delay hits your Core Web Vitals directly.
4. Skipping mobile-first design. Google indexes and ranks your mobile version first, not desktop. We’ve audited sites scoring 90+ on desktop and barely 30 on mobile — that gap alone explains a lot of stalled rankings.
5. No caching or CDN strategy. Without a CDN, a visitor on the opposite coast from your server is pulling every asset the slow way, every time.
Real example: one audit we ran found a site’s mobile LCP sitting at 6.2 seconds — almost entirely from a single unoptimized banner image and zero caching. Fixing just those two things brought it under 2.5 seconds within a day.
The pattern we see over and over: businesses treat performance as a one-time fix instead of an ongoing habit. New plugins get added, uncompressed images get uploaded, and scores quietly creep back down within a few months.
None of this requires rebuilding your site from scratch. It requires a clear audit and a plan to work through the highest-impact issues first.
Want the full breakdown? We cover all 10 mistakes — plus a quick-reference table of typical load-time impact and fixes — in the full post: 10 Website Performance Mistakes Killing Your Google Rankings in 2026
